revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Maekawa_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=前川神社| 画像= 所在地=[[埼玉県]][[川口市]][[前川町]]3-374 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 50 | 緯度秒 = 27.146 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 47.837 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP| 祭神=[[多岐都比売命]]
[[多紀理毘売命]]
[[狭依毘売命]]
外3柱| 社格=| 創建=不明| 例祭=[[10月17日]]}} '''前川神社'''（まえかわじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[川口市]]にある[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== [[多岐都比売命]],[[多紀理毘売命]],[[狭依毘売命]],外３柱を祀る。 ==歴史== [[1512年]][[7月15日]]の[[落雷]]による[[火災]]によって社殿や古記録を焼失したため、創建年は不明である。 神像は元々[[文蔵村]](現：[[さいたま市]][[南区 (さいたま市)|南区]][[文蔵]])に祀られていたが[[洪水]]で流され、この地に堰き止められたことから、「堰神社」として創建され、後に「勢貴社」に改められた。[[1873年]]に[[前川村]]の村社となり、島在家神明社、山王社などを合祀した。[[1965年]]に「前川神社」に改称。[[1978年]][[4月5日]]、内本殿が川口市指定文化財に指定された[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf 広報かわぐち2014.4-31頁]。 『新編武蔵風土記稿』に、前川神社について「勢貴明神社、祭神、多気津姫命、水災守護のため勧請」と書かれている。 ==行事== [[10月17日]]に例祭がある[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ 埼玉県神社庁ホームページ-前川神社]。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まえかわしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:川口市の歴史]]

{{神社| 名称=前川神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[埼玉県]][[川口市]][[前川町]]3-374 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 50 | 緯度秒 = 27.146 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 47.837 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP| 祭神=[[多岐都比売命]]

{{Shrine |Name = Maekawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[ja:前川神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Maekawa Shrine |Image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:前川神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Maekawa Shrine |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:前川神社]]

In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:前川神社]]

On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:bump]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:前川神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Maekawa Shrine |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:bump]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:前川神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion {{Shrine |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Maekawa Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:前川神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-qq_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-qq_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goqqesses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stopped in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-qq_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goqqesses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and stoqqed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Maekawa Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: tier 5]][[Category: {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Maekawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == Torii Gate {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市|12=draft}}, Maekawa 3-chome 49-1, Saitama Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命|12=draft}}, and three other deities |Rank = |Foundation = Unknown |Main Festival = October 17 |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = left }} '''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kawaguchi, Saitama|ja|川口市|12=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture. == Deities == The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=draft}}, specifically: * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命|12=draft}} The associated shrine houses: * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == History == The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a {{ill|Lightning strike|ja|落雷|12=draft}} fire on July 15, 1512. The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村|12=draft}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Saitama|ja|南区 (さいたま市)|12=draft}}. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31] The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding." == Festivals == The main festival is held annually on October 17.[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ Maekawa Shrine] - Official Website * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Maekawa Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawaguchi, Saitama]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Maekawa Shrine]] [[ja:前川神社]] [[sn:aekawa Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Maekawa Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=前川神社 |画像=[[File:Maekawa Jinja.JPG|250px]]
鳥居 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|50|27.9|N|139|42|47.6|E}}}} |所在地=[[埼玉県]][[川口市]][[前川 (川口市)|前川]]3丁目49番1号 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 50 | 緯度秒 = 27.9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 47.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神=[[多岐都比売命]]
[[多紀理毘売命]]
[[狭依毘売命]]
外3柱 |社格= |創建=不明 |例祭=[[10月17日]] |地図=Japan Saitama |ラベル位置=left }} '''前川神社'''（まえかわじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[川口市]][[前川 (川口市)|前川]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 本殿に勢貴大明神、（いわゆる[[宗像三女神]]、[[多岐都比売命]],[[多紀理毘売命]],[[狭依毘売命]]）を祀る。 相殿に[[天照大御神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[菅原道真]]の3柱神を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[1512年]][[7月15日]]の[[落雷]]による[[火災]]によって社殿や古記録を焼失したため、創建年は不明である。 神像は元々[[文蔵村]](現：[[さいたま市]][[南区 (さいたま市)|南区]][[文蔵]])に祀られていたが[[洪水]]で流され、この地に堰き止められたことから「堰神社」として創建され、後に「勢貴社」に改められた。[[1873年]]に[[前川村]]の村社となり、島在家神明社、山王社などを合祀した。[[1965年]]に「前川神社」に改称。[[1978年]][[4月5日]]、内本殿が川口市指定文化財に指定された[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf 広報かわぐち2014.4-31頁]。 『新編武蔵風土記稿』に、前川神社について「勢貴明神社、祭神、多気津姫命、水災守護のため勧請」と書かれている。 == 行事 == [[10月17日]]に例祭がある[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ 埼玉県神社庁ホームページ-前川神社]。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ 前川神社] - 公式サイト * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まえかわしんしや}} [[Category:川口市の神社]]

外3柱 |社格= |創建=不明 |例祭=[[10月17日]] |地図=Japan Saitama |ラベル位置=left }} '''前川神社'''（まえかわじんじゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[川口市]][[前川 (川口市)|前川]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 祭神 == 本殿に勢貴大明神、（いわゆる[[宗像三女神]]、[[多岐都比売命]],[[多紀理毘売命]],[[狭依毘売命]]）を祀る。 相殿に[[天照大御神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[菅原道真]]の3柱神を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[1512年]][[7月15日]]の[[落雷]]による[[火災]]によって社殿や古記録を焼失したため、創建年は不明である。 神像は元々[[文蔵村]](現：[[さいたま市]][[南区 (さいたま市)|南区]][[文蔵]])に祀られていたが[[洪水]]で流され、この地に堰き止められたことから「堰神社」として創建され、後に「勢貴社」に改められた。[[1873年]]に[[前川村]]の村社となり、島在家神明社、山王社などを合祀した。[[1965年]]に「前川神社」に改称。[[1978年]][[4月5日]]、内本殿が川口市指定文化財に指定された[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf 広報かわぐち2014.4-31頁]。 『新編武蔵風土記稿』に、前川神社について「勢貴明神社、祭神、多気津姫命、水災守護のため勧請」と書かれている。 == 行事 == [[10月17日]]に例祭がある[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ 埼玉県神社庁ホームページ-前川神社]。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.maekawajinja.com/ 前川神社] - 公式サイト * {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まえかわしんしや}} [[Category:川口市の神社]]

'''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kawaguchi, Saitama|Kawaguchi, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture.

== Deities ==
The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, specifically:
* {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
* {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayoirihime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}

The associated shrine houses:
* {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
* {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}

== History ==
The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning strike]] fire on July 15, 1512.

The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunzō Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, now part of [[:en:Minami-ku, Saitama|Minami-ku, Saitama]]. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). 

In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.<ref>[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31]</ref>

The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding."

== Festivals ==
The main festival is held annually on October 17.<ref>[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine]</ref>

== History ==
The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning strike]] fire on July 15, 1512.

The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunzō Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, now part of [[:en:Minami-ku, Saitama|Minami-ku, Saitama]]. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). 

In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.<ref>[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31]</ref>

The main festival is held annually on October 17.<ref>[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine]</ref>

'''Maekawa Shrine''' (前川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kawaguchi, Saitama|Kawaguchi, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture.

== Deities ==
The main hall enshrines the deities known as {{ill|Munakata Sanjojin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Go11esses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjojin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, specifically:
* {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}
* {{ill|Tagirihime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagirihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
* {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Sayoirihime|ja|狭依毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayoirihime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}

The associated shrine houses:
* {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}
* {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}

== History ==
The exact year of the shrine’s foundation is unknown due to the destruction of its original records and buildings in a [[:en:Lightning strike|Lightning strike]] fire on July 15, 1512.

The deity statues were originally enshrined in {{ill|Bunzō Village|ja|文蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunzō Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, now part of [[:en:Minami-ku, Saitama|Minami-ku, Saitama]]. However, following a flood, the statues were carried by the waters and sto11ed in the present location, where a shrine was built and initially named "Sekijinja" (堰神社). It was later renamed "Sekisha" (勢貴社). 

In 1873, the shrine became a village shrine of Maekawa Village. It incorporated several smaller shrines, including Shimazaike Shinmeisha and Sannosha. In 1965, it was renamed "Maekawa Shrine." On April 5, 1978, its inner main hall (内本殿) was designated as a cultural property by Kawaguchi City.<ref>[http://www.city.kawaguchi.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01050038/attach/P30-31.pdf Kawaguchi City Newsletter 2014, p.31]</ref>

The ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'' describes Maekawa Shrine as follows: "Sekimei Shrine, enshrining Tagitsuhime no Mikoto, established for protection against flooding."

== Festivals ==
The main festival is held annually on October 17.<ref>[http://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8864/ Saitama Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Maekawa Shrine]</ref>

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External Links ==

* {{Twitter|MaekawaJinja_SK}}
